'''Matthew Nicholson''' (born 18 July 2003) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Background==
Born on 18 July 2003, in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], Nicholson played his amateur rugby league for [[Siddal A.R.L.F.C.|Siddal]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Getting to know Astley & Nicholson |url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2022/05/12/getting-to-know-astley-nicholson/ |website=Wigan Warriors |access-date=8 July 2022 |date=12 May 2022}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Wigan===
Nicholson started his professional career at [[Wigan Warriors]]. He made his senior debut in 2021 on loan at [[Newcastle Thunder]], and was loaned out again to the club at the start of the 2022 season. In May 2022, Nicholson made his [[Super League]] debut for Wigan, scoring two tries in a 22–32 defeat against [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Wigan fall short against Giants |url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2022/05/12/wigan-fall-short-against-giants/ |website=Wigan Warriors |access-date=8 July 2022 |date=12 May 2022}}</ref>

===Warrington===
In June 2022, [[Warrington Wolves]] announced the signing of Nicholson on a three-and-a-half-year contract.<ref>{{cite web |title=Matty Nicholson; Warrington Wolves sign Wigan youngster |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/20238731.matty-nicholson-warrington-wolves-sign-wigan-youngster/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=8 July 2022 |date=29 June 2022}}</ref> Nicholson played 18 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref> On 8 June 2024, Nicholson played in Warrington's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Canberra Raiders===
On 12 July 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] in the NRL on a three-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=12 Jul 2024|accessdate=4 Oct 2024|title=Nicholson to leave Warrington for Canberra|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce5870rr532o}}</ref> In round 2 of the [[2025 NRL season]], Nicholson made his club debut for Canberra and scored two tries as they defeated [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]] 32-22.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/canberra-raiders-shock-brisbane-broncos-as-ex-warrington-wolves-star-matty-nicholson-grabs-a-double/|title=Canberra Raiders shock Brisbane Broncos as ex-Warrington Wolves star Matty Nicholson grabs a double|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>
Nicholson played 11 matches for Canberra in the [[2025 NRL season]] as the club claimed the [[Minor Premiership]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2025-the-mole-exclusive-canberra-raiders-season-review-20250924-p5mxr2.html/|title=The Mole's season review: 'Immediate job' for Raiders as unfair 'chokers' tag threatens to cloud long summer|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>
